templates.text: You see {echoText} depicted in the stonework. Older than it should be. Much older.
{echoText}. The dungeon hoards knowledge of it. You just took a piece.
The shadow in the corner is the wrong shape. It resembles {echoText}. The dungeon collects the forms of things it finds interesting.
{echoText} was made here, or something like it was. The techniques are in the walls — diagrams scratched by hands that no longer exist.
The walls know {echoText}. They have absorbed the knowledge of it the way old stone absorbs everything — without discrimination, without comment.
{echoText}. The dungeon's catalogue updates. Cross-referenced, filed, added to the index of things whose function and history are now held in your memory.
Something like {echoText} has been in this dungeon before. The stone around you carries the faint impression of its presence — a shape the walls learned and kept.
Dust on the floor holds the outline of {echoText}. Not placed there — accumulated there. The dungeon notes what you spend time near.
